A robust server-side "back up all", BombVault no longer stops itself, and a full-codebase hardening pass on backup/restore reliability. From forum testing of a "select all" backup, plus an internal audit.

## Added
- **Server-side batch backup.** "Back up selected" now kicks off a single request that backs up every selected container **on the server**, one after another, fully independent of your browser — close the tab and it keeps running. Progress streams over SSE: each container shows its own bar, with an overall batch indicator on the Containers page (it reconnects if you reopen the tab). Only one batch runs at a time. New env `BOMBVAULT_SELF_CONTAINER` (set in the template) pins self-detection for host-network setups.

## Fixed
- **A manual backup no longer dies when the browser does.** Each backup was driven by the HTTP request that started it, so closing the tab, losing the connection, or stopping the container the BombVault UI runs in (e.g. a Chromium container) cancelled the running backup mid-stream. Container, VM and flash backups now run under a detached context and survive the client going away.
- **BombVault no longer backs up — and stops — itself.** "Select all" included BombVault's own container; reaching it stopped the container (stop → backup → start), killing the process and the whole run. Its own container is now detected and refused for backup (its settings are recovered separately via **Discover**), excluded from "select all", and shown with a short note instead of a backup button.
- **Restore no longer tears down the target for a doomed restore.** Both container and VM restore now verify the snapshot is readable **before** anything is stopped/removed/destroyed, so a missing snapshot or an unreadable repo aborts with the container/VM still intact. Container restore also aborts if the old container can't be removed, instead of overwriting a still-running container's data.
- **No more silent empty backups.** If a container that previously captured data suddenly resolves to no paths (its appdata share isn't mounted, or a path is misconfigured), the backup is refused instead of being recorded as a successful empty backup that overwrites the stored path list. (A first backup of a new/stateless container is unaffected.)
- **More faithful container restore.** Recreate now preserves namespace/isolation and resource settings that were silently dropped — `PidMode`, `IpcMode`, `UsernsMode`, group-add, sysctls, tmpfs, extra hosts, ulimits, cgroup parent — and **all** networks (not just the primary), including a pinned MAC even when the container uses DHCP.
- **Scheduler can't be taken down by one bad job.** A panic in a single scheduled backup is now contained and logged instead of crashing the whole process (which silently stopped all schedules and the web UI).
- **Notification failures no longer leak secrets to the log.** A failed webhook/Healthchecks send used to log the full URL, which carries a secret token in the path; the URL is now stripped from the logged error.

## Changed
- **Hardening & polish:** logins are rate-limited; the file-browser endpoint is scoped to its container; boot fails fast if `/config` isn't a writable mount (instead of silently persisting to the container's ephemeral layer); the restic progress reader tolerates very long file paths; the schedule "last successful backup" check is more precise. The Settings page now saves each card independently (saving one card no longer commits another card's unsaved edits), and more of the UI is translated.

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.7.0` (also `:2`, `:2.7`, `:latest`) and `docker.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.7.0`, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
